title: U-A-Goose
text:
We are the U-a-Goose (GrOup Of Software Engineering) group at University of Alberta, working at the intersection of:

Foundation Models for Software — building strong foundation models that can understand and reason about complex software systems at scale
SE4AI — applying and evaluating AI techniques within software engineering workflows

We analyze software artefacts — code, execution traces, bug reports, Q&A posts, and developer networks — to build automated tools that improve system reliability, developer productivity, and security.

  

“Your Lab Motto Here”


🤝 Global Collaborations
Microsoft Research, Adobe, NUS, NTU, and many more institutions worldwide.


📄 Top-Tier Publications
ICSE, FSE, ASE, ISSTA · IJCAI, AAAI · IEEE S&P, ESORICS



🎓 We are recruiting! We are looking for passionate PhD students and Research Engineers. Contact Prof. Zhou Yang for more information.






[2026.03] Dr. Zhou Yang win the MSR Outstanding Doctoral Reserch Award🏆! There is only one awardee this year.
[2026.01] One paper (on automatically protecting critical software operations in TEE) is accepted by IEEE Transactions on Software Engineering!

title: Team
text:
Assistant Professor at the University of Alberta, Fellow at Amii (Alberta Machine Intelligence Institute)
Dr. Zhou Yang is an Assistant Professor at the University of Alberta and a Fellow at the Alberta Machine Intelligence Institute (Amii). He received his Ph.D. from Singapore Management University and his M.Sc. from University College London. His research focuses on building strong foundation models for complex software systems, developing trusted AI for software engineering, and analyzing the socio-technical impact of AI on open-source software and developer productivity.
📧 zy25@ualberta.ca | 🔗 Google Scholar | 🌐 Homepage
